name: 44 Pro PRX Signature Elite Wildchild 11.5" Infield Glove
descriptionShort: A custom-tier I-web infielder from a boutique American glove maker — top-shelf leather with a standard fit geometry and a shallow pocket that transfers quickly.
notes: 44 Pro's custom manufacturing gives this a different feel from production-line gloves — the leather break-in is premium but responds well to consistent use. Expect 4–5 weeks. Standard fitProfile means no sizing surprises.

name: Akadema Pro Soft Elite ANO315 11.5" Infield Glove
descriptionShort: A solid mid-range infielder with forgiving geometry and a break-in curve that won't demand months of conditioning.
notes: Good pick for recreational players who want real leather without a premium project. Runs standard in fit — no need to adjust on sizing.

name: All Star S7 Elite 34" Catcher's Mitt
descriptionShort: A serious receiver's tool built around a deep, secure pocket with the durability to handle a full season behind the plate.
notes: Plan on 4–6 weeks of real conditioning before this mitt reaches its peak feel. Well-regarded by travel and high school catchers who want pro-grade construction without the Rawlings price tag.

name: Emery Special Edition Cordura 11.5" Infield Glove
descriptionShort: A clean mid-grade infielder from a small-batch American maker — real leather, a shallow pocket, and a fit that runs slightly snug for a locked-in feel.
notes: Emery is a lower-profile brand worth knowing — solid construction at a reasonable price. The slightly snug fit is intentional for this model. Break-in is moderate; 1–2 weeks and it's game-ready.

name: Emery Special Edition Cordura 11.75" Utility Glove
descriptionShort: A balanced mid-grade utility glove from a small-batch American maker — a single-post web and medium pocket that plays credibly across multiple infield spots.
notes: An unusual web choice for a utility design that works well for players splitting time between SS and 3B. Slightly snug fit at the wrist — standard for this maker's construction. Moderate break-in, game-ready in about 2 weeks.

name: Marucci Acadia 12.5" First Base Mitt
descriptionShort: A lightweight, accessible first base mitt designed for younger players learning the position — soft enough to close confidently, sized for developing hands.
notes: Ready to use almost immediately — no real conditioning needed. The narrower fit works well for youth players, but adults with average-sized hands may want to size up or move to the A1000 series.

name: Mizuno Franchise GXC90 33.5" Catcher's Mitt
descriptionShort: A mid-tier catcher's mitt with accessible break-in and solid construction — built for the receiver who's serious about the position but not ready for a premium project.
notes: Good entry point for high school catchers who want real mitt geometry without a demanding break-in. Closes willingly for a catcher's mitt — you can be game-ready in 2–3 weeks. Runs standard in fit throughout.

name: Mizuno Franchise 11.5" Infield Glove
descriptionShort: A dependable mid-tier infielder with real leather, a shallow I-web pocket, and a break-in curve that doesn't demand a long-term commitment.
notes: One of the more accessible mid-tier baseball infield gloves in the catalog. Standard fit and wrist geometry — no sizing quirks. Break-in takes about 2 weeks with regular use.

name: Mizuno Franchise 12.5" Outfield Glove
descriptionShort: A mid-tier outfielder with an H-web and enough pocket depth for confident fly ball work — good leather at a practical price.
notes: The H-web and moderate pocket make this one of the better value outfield options in this lineup. Standard fit throughout. Break-in is mild — 1–2 weeks and you're game-ready.

name: Mizuno Franchise 12" Utility Glove
descriptionShort: A balanced mid-tier utility glove with a basket web and neutral pocket depth — the all-positions design philosophy at a practical price point.
notes: One of the better all-around options for players who cover 2–3 spots. The basket web and neutral pocket depth mean nothing about this glove fights you at a new position. Breaks in within 2 weeks.

name: Mizuno Franchise GXF90 12.5" First Base Mitt
descriptionShort: A reliable mid-tier first baseman's mitt with good scoop geometry and leather quality that holds its shape through a full season.
notes: Moderately stiff out of the box — 2–3 weeks of regular use and this mitt settles in well. Standard fit at both wrist and stall means it works for most adult hands without modification.

name: Mizuno Pro 7100D 12.75" Outfield Glove
descriptionShort: A top-tier outfield pattern in premium leather — deep pocket, modified trap web, and pro-level construction for the serious outfielder who plans to keep this glove for years.
notes: Stiffness of 4 means this needs real work before game use — plan on 4–6 weeks. The premium leather pays dividends in feel and longevity. Standard geometry throughout; sizing is predictable.

name: Mizuno Pro 3000 13" First Base Mitt
descriptionShort: A pro-grade first baseman's mitt in premium leather — large pattern construction for experienced 1B players who want a career glove with maximum scooping range.
notes: The 13" size is noticeably large — excellent for catching low throws but takes adjustment if you're used to 12.5". Plan on 4–5 weeks of conditioning. This one develops a beautiful shape over time.

name: Mizuno MVP Prime 1278 12.75" Outfield Glove
descriptionShort: A deep-pocket outfield glove with an H-web for sun visibility — built to handle range plays at a mid-premium price point.
notes: H-web distinguishes this from the modified trap variant — better for players who lose the ball in sun or lights on routine fly balls. Same stiffness, same break-in timeline (2–3 weeks). Works credibly in slowpitch.

name: Mizuno MVP Prime 1277 12.75" Outfield Glove
descriptionShort: A modified trap-web outfield glove calibrated for both baseball and slowpitch — deep pocket, real leather, and a break-in curve that's manageable for most players.
notes: Stiffness sits at 3, so plan for 2–3 weeks before this fully settles. The modified trap web offers good ball retention while maintaining flexibility. Plays well in slowpitch given the deeper pocket.

name: Mizuno Pro Select 33.5" Catcher's Mitt
descriptionShort: A premium receiver's tool in quality leather — deep closed-web pocket and structural integrity designed for heavy use behind the plate.
notes: Break-in runs 3–4 weeks — not as demanding as a HoH mitt but requires real work. The closed web and deep pocket are calibrated for experienced catchers who want to feel every pitch come into the pocket.

name: Mizuno Pro Select 70D 12.75" Outfield Glove
descriptionShort: A premium outfield glove with a deep pocket and quality leather — designed around experienced outfielders who want reliable catch security in a well-built package.
notes: An unusual single-post web on an outfield glove — some outfielders prefer the rigidity it offers over H-web or trap designs. Stiffness is manageable at 3; 2–4 weeks to break in. Crosses over well to slowpitch.

name: Mizuno Prospect PowerClose 11.5" Infield Glove
descriptionShort: A forgiving youth infielder with a basket web that helps younger players pocket the ball more reliably on fielding plays.
notes: Slightly deeper pocket than smaller sizes in the same line makes this more versatile for players splitting time between infield and pitcher. Closes easily and requires no real break-in.

name: Mizuno Prospect PowerClose 10" Infield Glove
descriptionShort: An ultra-soft entry-level infielder built around a snap-close design — engineered so developing hands can actually squeeze it shut.
notes: Purpose-built for the youngest players — the 10" size and snap-close construction make it one of the easiest starter gloves to control. Don't overthink the closed web for a 9-year-old infielder.

name: Mizuno Prospect PowerClose 11" Infield Glove
descriptionShort: The soft, snap-close youth infielder — enough glove to learn the position, light enough to not fight it.
notes: The step up from the 10" version, with an I-web that introduces players to the classic infield shape. Still closes easily for youth hand strength. Size up if the player is on the larger side for their age.

name: Mizuno Prospect Select 11" Infield Glove
descriptionShort: A compact youth infield glove — snug fit, very shallow pocket, and easy close for developing players learning to transfer quickly out of the glove.
notes: Good youth transition glove for players 10–13. The very shallow pocket introduces young infielders to the quick-release concept naturally. Fit runs snug; appropriate for smaller hands. No real break-in needed.

name: Mizuno Prospect Select 12" Utility Glove
descriptionShort: A step up from entry-level — mid-grade leather and a versatile pattern that grows with a young player across multiple positions.
notes: Good bridge glove for players 12–14 who've outgrown entry-level but aren't ready for a premium break-in project. The 12" size and basket web play naturally at pitcher or utility.

name: Mizuno Samurai GXC95 33" Catcher's Mitt
descriptionShort: A lighter, more accessible youth-to-teen catcher's mitt with a deep round pocket and fast close — built for developing catchers who need a real mitt without a demanding break-in.
notes: Closes quickly for a catcher's mitt — good for younger players still building hand strength. The slightly snug fit dimensions make it a natural fit for smaller hands. Holds its shape through a full season.

name: Nike Vapor Elite Acuna 12.75" Outfield Glove
descriptionShort: A premium outfield glove anchored by an H-web and deep pocket — built for range and designed to hold line drives hit to the gap.
notes: Stiffness sits at 3, so plan for 2–3 weeks of conditioning before it reaches its natural break point. The H-web offers better sun visibility than a trap design — worth noting for CF players.

name: Nokona Hunting Season 11.5" Infield Glove
descriptionShort: American-made premium leather in a shallow I-web infield cut — a glove that rewards patient break-in work with an unmatched natural feel over time.
notes: This leather molds to your hand over time rather than just softening up — plan on 3–5 weeks and avoid shortcuts. Standard fit geometry throughout; sizing is predictable.

name: Nokona Hunting Season 12.75" Outfield Glove
descriptionShort: American-made outfield leather in a premium colorway — full-grain hide built for players who want a glove that outlasts their playing career.
notes: Nokona's outfield pattern demands patience — 4–6 weeks of real conditioning. Standard fit throughout. The quality of hide means every hour of break-in pays off for years.

name: Nokona Hunting Season 11.75" Utility Glove
descriptionShort: American-made premium leather in a flexible utility cut — full-grain hide with a balanced pocket that travels comfortably across positions.
notes: The H-web and medium pocket depth make this the most versatile model in this collection. Break-in is real at stiffness 4 — plan 4–5 weeks. Don't steam it; this leather develops best with repeated use and conditioning oil.

name: Nokona S1 All American 11.5" Infield Glove
descriptionShort: A more accessible entry into American leather — quicker break-in than most premium American-made models, with a snug stall and shallow pocket built for fast hands.
notes: Uses a softer, more pre-conditioned hide than flagship American collections — breaks in in 1–2 weeks, not months. The fit runs slightly snug which pairs well with its quick-close design. A good gateway into American-made gloves.

name: Rawlings Heart of the Hide ContoUR Fit 11.5" Baseball Glove: PROR204U-2N
descriptionShort: A premium ContoUR Fit infielder with Heart of the Hide leather, a shallow pocket, and a snug hand opening for smaller-handed players ready to move into top-tier construction.
notes: The ContoUR build runs narrow through the wrist and finger stalls, so this fits younger players and smaller adult hands especially well. Still a premium-leather glove, but the break-in is more approachable than a full-stiff HoH project. Good quick-transfer shape for second base, shortstop, and third.

name: Rawlings Heart of the Hide R2G ContoUR Fit 11.25" Baseball Glove: PROR312U-2R
descriptionShort: A premium 11.25" ContoUR Fit infielder with Heart of the Hide leather and R2G softening, built for smaller-handed players who want a quick-transfer pattern without a long premium break-in.
notes: ContoUR geometry keeps the wrist opening and finger stalls snug, which helps younger and smaller-handed players control the glove. The R2G treatment makes this much easier to close than a standard HoH, with most of the break-in done after a short run of catch. Best for middle infield work.

name: Rawlings R9 Contour 11.25" Baseball Glove: R91125U-2BP
descriptionShort: A youth-friendly R9 ContoUR infielder with full-grain leather, a snug hand opening, and an 11.25" I-web pattern that makes sense for developing middle infielders.
notes: Built for adolescent and teen hands, this runs narrower at the wrist and stalls than a standard R9. Break-in is short and closure comes easily, so it works well for players stepping into a real leather glove without fighting it. The 11.25" size is a good fit for second base and shortstop.

name: Rawlings R9 ContoUR Fit 11" Baseball Glove: R9110U-2BM
descriptionShort: A very youth-focused R9 ContoUR infielder with full-grain leather, an 11" I-web pattern, and a snug fit that smaller hands can actually control.
notes: This is one of the more youth-specific baseball gloves in the Rawlings lineup. The narrow wrist and smaller overall pattern help younger infielders close it cleanly, and the break-in stays short. Best for players who are not ready for a larger teen or adult infield glove.

name: Rawlings R9 Contour 11.5" Baseball Glove: R9115U-4BCB
descriptionShort: A youth-friendly R9 ContoUR glove with a pitcher-friendly web, easy break-in, and a snug hand fit that also works for young utility players.
notes: The ContoUR fit keeps this manageable for smaller hands despite the 11.5" size. Break-in is mild and closure comes easily, which matters for younger players still building hand strength. The trap-style web gives a little more ball concealment for pitchers while still working for general field use.

name: Rawlings Encore 32" Baseball Catcher's Mitt: ECCM32-23B
descriptionShort: A youth-accessible 32" catcher's mitt with all-leather construction, easy break-in, and a compact size that suits developing backstops.
notes: Encore mitts are built to get on the field sooner than premium catcher's mitts, and the 32" circumference is easier for adolescent and teen catchers to manage. The closed web and deep pocket give good receiving security without the fight of a stiff pro mitt. Some adult hands can fit it, but it is clearly sized with younger catchers in mind.

name: Rawlings Gamer XLE Speed Shell 32.5" Baseball Catcher's Mitt: GXLESP13BSS
descriptionShort: A mid-tier catcher's mitt with a lighter Speed Shell back, pro-style 32.5" size, and a quicker break-in than a premium receiver's mitt.
notes: The Speed Shell backing trims some weight while keeping the feel more substantial than entry-level mitts. Standard hand fit throughout, so this suits teen and adult catchers better than smaller ContoUR models. Good option for players who want a full-size mitt without Heart of the Hide stiffness.

name: Rawlings Gamer G204 11.5" Infield Glove
descriptionShort: A reliable mid-tier infielder with a shallow pocket and classic I-web — dependable leather that breaks in without a major time investment.
notes: Good pick for recreational or high school players who want the I-web shape without committing to a HoH break-in. Runs true to standard fit — no sizing adjustments needed.

name: Rawlings Gamer XLE Speed Shell 11.5" Baseball Glove: GXLE314-2BSS
descriptionShort: A reliable mid-tier infield glove with a shallow pocket, I-web, and lighter Speed Shell backing for players who want easier break-in and less glove weight.
notes: The Speed Shell back helps this feel lighter in motion, but it still plays like a real leather infield glove. Break-in is manageable and the standard fit should work for most teen and adult hands. Good for recreational through high school use.

name: Rawlings Gamer XLE Speed Shell Kris Bryant 12.25" Baseball Glove: GXLEKB17-6BSS
descriptionShort: A mid-tier utility glove in the Kris Bryant pattern, pairing Gamer XLE leather with a lighter Speed Shell back and a 12.25" H-web shape that moves cleanly between dirt and grass.
notes: The KB17 pattern gives this more versatility than a true infield-only glove, with enough pocket to handle corner outfield work. Speed Shell keeps the feel lighter on the hand, and break-in stays manageable. Best for players splitting time across multiple positions.

name: Rawlings Gamer ContoUR 32.5" Baseball Catcher's Mitt: GCM325U-23B
descriptionShort: A snug-fitting 32.5" catcher's mitt for youth and smaller-handed backstops who want a game-ready mitt with more structure than entry-level options.
notes: The Gamer ContoUR line runs noticeably narrow at the wrist and hand stall, and that is a real benefit behind the plate. The 32.5" circumference gives developing catchers enough surface area without feeling oversized. Break-in is mild and closure comes easier than premium mitts.

name: Rawlings Gamer ContoUR G105U 10.5" Infield Glove
descriptionShort: A very small, snug youth infield glove built for the youngest players in the game — compact, light, and narrow enough for small hands to actually control.
notes: The 10.5" size and very snug geometry make this one of the most youth-specific gloves in the catalog. Designed for players 7–10. Closes easily enough for developing hand strength. No real break-in needed.

name: Rawlings Gamer ContoUR G206U 12" Outfield Glove
descriptionShort: A snug-cut youth outfield glove with a real H-web and decent pocket depth — narrow geometry and modest leather for players transitioning to the outfield with confidence.
notes: The -2 fitProfile and wristOpening are notable for an outfield glove — this runs very snug. Built for youth and smaller-handed teen players. Breaks in in 1–2 weeks.

name: Rawlings Gamer ContoUR 10" Youth Baseball Glove: G100U-3B
descriptionShort: A very youth-specific utility glove with a tiny 10" profile, snug ContoUR fit, and simple basket-web design that makes first catches and basic fielding easier to learn.
notes: This is built for the youngest players in the game. The very narrow hand opening and easy closure help small hands actually control the glove, and the utility shape works across multiple spots while a player is still learning. Little to no break-in is needed.

name: Rawlings Gamer ContoUR GDCTU 12.25" First Base Mitt
descriptionShort: A snug-fitting youth and teen first base mitt with real leather construction and the scoop geometry that makes plays at the bag feel natural.
notes: Runs very snug — sized for smaller hands. Not the right call for an average adult 1B player, but an excellent fit for teen players learning the position. Break-in is mild; game-ready in 1–2 weeks.

name: Rawlings Gold Glove Elite 11.5" Baseball Glove: GGE115MTCBPT
descriptionShort: A mid-tier 11.5" pitcher or infield glove with quality leather, a modified trap web, and a quick path to game-ready use.
notes: Gold Glove Elite sits in the sweet spot for players who want better leather than an entry model without stepping into a long premium break-in. The modified trap web gives pitchers some concealment while still working for infield utility use. Standard fit throughout and easy to get comfortable with.

name: Rawlings Heart of the Hide PROAR35 34" Catcher's Mitt
descriptionShort: The Heart of the Hide catcher's mitt — top-tier leather, deep pocket, and the structural authority that serious receivers build careers around.
notes: This is a real break-in commitment — 4–6 weeks minimum, no shortcuts with steam or microwave. Standard fit geometry means sizing is predictable. Buy this when you're ready to invest the time it takes to own a genuinely great mitt.

name: Rawlings Heart of the Hide PROCM335 33.5" Catcher's Mitt
descriptionShort: Heart of the Hide leather in a 33.5" closed-web pattern — premium construction for the receiver who wants a slightly more compact fit without giving up catch authority.
notes: The 33.5" size plays slightly more compact than the 34" models — preferred by catchers with smaller hands or those transitioning from a smaller mitt. Same demanding break-in as the full HoH line; plan on 4–6 weeks.

name: Rawlings Heart of the Hide PROYM4 34" Catcher's Mitt
descriptionShort: A Heart of the Hide 34" catcher's mitt built for the serious receiver who demands top-shelf leather and the security of a deep, full-round pocket.
notes: Full HoH conditioning commitment — 4–6 weeks before this reaches its best feel. The closed web and deep pocket construction is designed around the advanced receiver's style. Standard fit; no quirks on sizing.

name: Rawlings Heart of the Hide PROYM4 34" Catcher's Mitt
descriptionShort: A Heart of the Hide 34" catcher's mitt built for the serious receiver who demands top-shelf leather and the security of a deep, full-round pocket.
notes: Full HoH conditioning commitment — 4–6 weeks before this reaches its best feel. The closed web and deep pocket construction is designed around the advanced receiver's style. Standard fit; no quirks on sizing.

name: Rawlings Heart of the Hide 32.5" Baseball Catcher's Mitt: PRORCM325U-23C
descriptionShort: A premium 32.5" catcher's mitt in Heart of the Hide leather, built for serious backstops who want top-tier materials in a more manageable fit.
notes: This model runs snug through the wrist, making it a better fit for smaller-handed catchers than full-size Heart of the Hide mitts. The 32.5" circumference keeps the profile compact, but the deep pocket and firm leather still give real catch security. Expect a shorter break-in than the stiffest HoH mitts, but it is still a premium piece.

name: Rawlings Heart of the Hide 33" Baseball Catcher's Mitt: PRORCM33-23CBP
descriptionShort: A full premium 33" catcher's mitt with Heart of the Hide leather, deep pocket security, and the kind of structure advanced receivers usually prefer.
notes: The 33" size plays a little more compact than 34" catcher's mitts, which can help receivers who want quicker glove control without giving up pro-level construction. This is still a real Heart of the Hide break-in commitment, closer to 4–6 weeks than a game-ready mitt. Standard fit with no hand-fit quirks.

name: Rawlings Heart of the Hide ColorSync 10.0 34" Catcher's Mitt: PRORCM43-23BEM
descriptionShort: A full-size 34" Heart of the Hide catcher's mitt built for advanced receivers who want maximum pocket depth and true pro-level structure.
notes: At 34", this is the largest, most substantial catcher build in this batch. It rewards strong hands and patience, with a longer Heart of the Hide break-in before the pocket really settles. Best for catchers who want full-round pocket security more than a compact feel.

name: Rawlings Heart of the Hide Mint Condition 33" Baseball Catcher's Mitt: PRORCM33-23MP
descriptionShort: A premium 33" catcher's mitt with Heart of the Hide leather and a compact pro-style build for receivers who want secure pocket depth without going full 34".
notes: The Mint Condition look is cosmetic. The underlying build is still a firm, serious Heart of the Hide catcher's mitt with a real 4–6 week break-in. Good fit for catchers who like a slightly more manageable profile than the biggest full-size mitts.

name: Rawlings Heart of the Hide 11.5" Baseball Glove: PROT204-2B
descriptionShort: The Heart of the Hide PRO204 — an I-web infield standard with a shallow pocket, top-tier leather, and the durability to survive a playing career.
notes: One of the most proven infield patterns in the Heart of the Hide lineup. The shallow pocket and I-web make it one of the faster-transfer premium gloves in the catalog. Break-in takes 4–6 weeks. Standard fit; sizing is predictable.

name: Rawlings Heart of the Hide ColorSync 8.0 Speed Shell 11.5" Baseball Glove: PRO934-2TS
descriptionShort: A premium 11.5" I-web infielder with Heart of the Hide leather and Speed Shell backing, built for quick transfers with a slightly lighter overall feel.
notes: The Speed Shell back trims some weight, but this still behaves like a true Heart of the Hide glove in structure and durability. Shallow pocket and I-web shape make it a natural middle infield pattern. Break-in remains on the longer side, around 4–6 weeks.

name: Rawlings Heart of the Hide Crossover 11.5" Baseball Glove: PROR204W-2XO
descriptionShort: The Heart of the Hide PRO204W — the wider-back variant of the classic PRO204 I-web pattern, with the same shallow pocket and top-tier leather in a more open hand design.
notes: The wider-back construction gives this a more open feel in the hand than a standard PRO204. The Crossover styling is cosmetic, not structural. Same premium leather, same quick-transfer infield shape, and the same 4–6 week Heart of the Hide break-in.

name: Rawlings Heart of the Hide Funburst 11.5" Baseball Glove: PROR204-2CBO
descriptionShort: The Heart of the Hide PRO204 — an I-web infield standard with a shallow pocket, top-tier leather, and the durability to survive a playing career.
notes: The Funburst finish is cosmetic. Underneath, this is still the classic PRO204 shape with a shallow pocket and quick-transfer I-web. Break-in takes 4–6 weeks, and the hand fit stays standard and predictable.

name: Rawlings Heart of the Hide Funburst 11.5" Baseball Glove: PROR204-2PUP
descriptionShort: The Heart of the Hide PRO204 — an I-web infield standard with a shallow pocket, top-tier leather, and the durability to survive a playing career.
notes: The Funburst finish changes the look, not the way the glove plays. This is still a proven middle-infield pattern with fast transfer behavior and full Heart of the Hide structure. Plan on a 4–6 week break-in.

name: Rawlings Heart of the Hide Funburst 11.5" Baseball Glove: PROR204-2RSS
descriptionShort: The Heart of the Hide PRO204 — an I-web infield standard with a shallow pocket, top-tier leather, and the durability to survive a playing career.
notes: This version keeps the same dependable PRO204 performance spec despite the brighter Funburst look. The shallow pocket and I-web make it one of the quicker premium infield shapes in the catalog. Full Heart of the Hide break-in applies.

name: Rawlings Heart of the Hide PRO204 11.5" Infield Glove
descriptionShort: The Heart of the Hide PRO204 — an I-web infield standard with a shallow pocket, top-tier leather, and the durability to survive a playing career.
notes: One of the most proven infield patterns in the HoH lineup. The shallow pocket and I-web make it one of the faster-transfer premium gloves in the catalog. Break-in takes 4–6 weeks. Standard fit; sizing is predictable.

name: Rawlings Heart of the Hide PRO204 11.5" Infield Glove
descriptionShort: The Heart of the Hide PRO204 — an I-web infield standard with a shallow pocket, top-tier leather, and the durability to survive a playing career.
notes: One of the most proven infield patterns in the HoH lineup. The shallow pocket and I-web make it one of the faster-transfer premium gloves in the catalog. Break-in takes 4–6 weeks. Standard fit; sizing is predictable.

name: Rawlings Heart of the Hide PRO204W 11.5" Infield Glove
descriptionShort: The Heart of the Hide PRO204W — the wider-back variant of the classic PRO204 I-web pattern, with the same shallow pocket and top-tier leather in a more open hand design.
notes: The 'W' designation indicates a wider back opening — same construction and break-in timeline as the standard PRO204, but with a more open feel in the hand stall. Standard fit everywhere except the back design. Break-in: 4–6 weeks.

name: Rawlings Heart of the Hide PRO205U 11.75" Infield Glove
descriptionShort: The Heart of the Hide PRO205U — the slightly snug variant of the 11.75" infield pattern for players who want HoH leather with a tighter, more controlled feel.
notes: The 'U' designation indicates a tighter stall geometry — runs slightly snug. Hits the middle ground between standard HoH geometry and the extreme narrowness of the NXT line. Full HoH break-in applies.

name: Rawlings Heart of the Hide PRO210U 10.75" Infield Glove
descriptionShort: A small, snug Heart of the Hide infielder — compact size with a very tight stall and shallow pocket for the quick-hands middle infielder who wants premium leather in a compact profile.
notes: The combination of small size, very tight fitProfile, and very shallow pocket makes this one of the most specialized infield gloves in the catalog. Best for SS or 2B players with smaller hands who prioritize transfer speed above everything. Full HoH break-in.

name: Rawlings Heart of the Hide PRO210U 10.75" Infield Glove
descriptionShort: A small, snug Heart of the Hide infielder — compact size with a very tight stall and shallow pocket for the quick-hands middle infielder who wants premium leather in a compact profile.
notes: The combination of small size, very tight fitProfile, and very shallow pocket makes this one of the most specialized infield gloves in the catalog. Best for SS or 2B players with smaller hands who prioritize transfer speed above everything. Full HoH break-in.

name: Rawlings Heart of the Hide PRO234U 11.5" Infield Glove
descriptionShort: The Heart of the Hide PRO234U — snug geometry, shallow pocket, and I-web construction in a 11.5" pattern for players who want HoH leather with a tighter feel.
notes: Runs slightly snug at both fit and wrist — consistent across the 'U' designations in the HoH line. Shallow pocket for quick transfer. Full HoH break-in: 4–6 weeks.

name: Rawlings Heart of the Hide PRO312 11.25" Infield Glove
descriptionShort: A compact Heart of the Hide infielder in the PRO312 pattern — small-size I-web with shallow pocket for 2B specialists who want premium leather at a smaller profile.
notes: The 11.25" size naturally runs lighter and more compact — good for smaller-handed 2B players who want true HoH construction. Standard fitProfile throughout. Full break-in (4–6 weeks) still applies.

name: Rawlings Heart of the Hide PRO314 11.5" Infield Glove
descriptionShort: The Heart of the Hide PRO314 I-web — a shallow-pocket infield standard in top-tier leather, built for players who want the classic HoH infield experience.
notes: The I-web version of the PRO314 is a pure quick-transfer infield glove — no concessions on pocket depth or web opening. Standard fit; full HoH break-in timeline of 4–6 weeks.

name: Rawlings Heart of the Hide PRONP4 11.5" Infield Glove
descriptionShort: The Heart of the Hide PRONP4 — a proven I-web infield pattern with a shallow pocket and top-tier leather that makes it one of the lineup's most trusted designs.
notes: PRONP4 is a well-regarded HoH pattern among serious infielders — clean I-web, reliable pocket shape, predictable break-in. Standard fit throughout. Plan on 4–6 weeks.

name: Rawlings Heart of the Hide 11.5" Baseball Glove: PROR204-2XPU
descriptionShort: The Heart of the Hide PRO204 — an I-web infield standard with a shallow pocket, top-tier leather, and the durability to survive a playing career.
notes: One of the most proven infield patterns in the Heart of the Hide lineup. The shallow pocket and I-web make it one of the faster-transfer premium gloves in the catalog. Break-in takes 4–6 weeks. Standard fit; sizing is predictable.

name: Rawlings Heart of the Hide 11.5" Baseball Glove: PROR314-2SY
descriptionShort: The Heart of the Hide PRO314 I-web — a shallow-pocket infield standard in top-tier leather, built for players who want the classic Heart of the Hide infield experience.
notes: The I-web version of the PRO314 is a pure quick-transfer infield glove with no concessions on pocket depth or web opening. Standard fit throughout. Full Heart of the Hide break-in applies, so plan on roughly 4–6 weeks.

name: Rawlings Heart of the Hide 11.5" Baseball Glove: PROR314U-2Y
descriptionShort: A premium 11.5" infielder with Heart of the Hide leather and a tighter hand fit for players who want top-tier materials in a snugger pattern.
notes: The U build runs narrower at the wrist and finger stalls than standard Heart of the Hide infielders. That makes it easier for smaller hands to control despite the premium leather. Break-in is shorter than a full-stiff HoH, but it is still more project glove than game-ready.

name: Rawlings Heart of the Hide 11.5" Baseball Glove: PROR934-2DSP
descriptionShort: A premium 11.5" I-web infielder with Heart of the Hide leather and a clean quick-transfer shape for competitive middle infield play.

name: Rawlings Heart of the Hide ColorSync 10.0 11.75" Baseball Glove: PROR315-32CBP

name: Rawlings Heart of the Hide PRO205 11.75" Infield Glove

notes: The two-piece closed web hides grip reading on the mound and offers more pocket stability than an open web — a natural choice for pitchers who also play 3B. Same HoH break-in commitment; 4–6 weeks.

name: Rawlings Heart of the Hide Mint Condition 11.75" Baseball Glove: PROR205-30MP

notes: This pattern makes sense for third base, pitcher, or infielders who simply prefer a more closed look. The Mint Condition styling is cosmetic. The 11.75" size keeps it versatile enough for multiple spots on the dirt, and full Heart of the Hide break-in applies.

notes: The extra length and deeper pocket help with fly-ball security, but the narrowed wrist and hand stall keep the glove from feeling loose. Good fit for teen outfielders or smaller-handed players who want an outfield pattern with a tighter connection. Break-in is shorter than premium pro-stock lines, but not fully game-ready out of the box.

name: Rawlings NXT 12.25" Baseball Glove: NXT207U-6G
descriptionShort: A snug-cut 12.25" NXT utility glove with an H-web and balanced pocket for players who move between the dirt, the corners, and shallow outfield.
notes: This is one of the more versatile shapes in the line, giving enough length for third base and utility work without losing infield feel. The tighter wrist and narrow stalls create a locked-in fit, especially for smaller adult or teen hands. Plan on a short break-in before it feels fully settled.

name: Rawlings NXT 12.25" Baseball First Base Mitt: NXTDCTU-10N
descriptionShort: A snug-fitting 12.25" first base mitt in pro-soft leather, built for younger or smaller-handed first basemen who want a more premium feel without a pro-level break-in.
notes: The narrowed wrist opening and hand stall make this more approachable than many adult first base mitts, which is why it works well for teens and developing first basemen. The single-post design and deeper pocket give secure scoop help around the bag. Break-in is manageable, but it still has more structure than entry-level mitts.

name: Rawlings Pro Preferred 33" Baseball Catcher's Mitt: PROSCM33-23BM
descriptionShort: A premium 33" Pro Preferred catcher's mitt with kip leather and a firm, secure pocket for catchers who want pro-grade feel in a slightly more compact size.
notes: The 33" circumference keeps the target compact and quick for transfers while still offering strong receive-and-stick security. Kip leather gives this line a lighter, smoother feel than Heart of the Hide, but it is still a serious break-in project. Standard fit through the hand with more structure than most mid-tier mitts.

name: Rawlings Pro Preferred 34" Baseball Catcher's Mitt: PROSCM43CBS
descriptionShort: A premium 34" Pro Preferred catcher's mitt with kip leather, a full-size receiving surface, and pro-level structure for advanced backstops.
notes: The 34" size gives this more surface area and pocket volume for receiving high-velocity pitching than the smaller catcher’s patterns. Kip leather makes the glove feel lighter and cleaner than steerhide, but the break-in is still substantial. Best for older catchers who want a traditional pro-style mitt.

name: Rawlings Pro Preferred 11.75" Baseball Glove: PROSCC4S
descriptionShort: A premium 11.75" Pro Preferred infielder with kip leather, an H-web, and a balanced pocket that leans toward third base and utility work.
notes: The extra visibility and slightly sturdier pocket shape of the H-web make this a good fit for players who like a little more glove around the ball than a shallow I-web model. Kip leather gives it a lighter, smoother feel than Heart of the Hide with a somewhat friendlier break-in. Standard fit; no hand-size quirks.

name: Rawlings Pro Preferred 11.5" Baseball Glove: RPROS314-2R
descriptionShort: The Pro Preferred PROS314, a premium kip-leather 11.5" infield glove with a shallow I-web pocket and the quick-transfer feel serious infielders look for.
notes: This is a classic small-pocket Rawlings infield build for second base and shortstop players who want pro-level leather with a slightly smoother feel than Heart of the Hide. Kip leather trims some weight and shortens the break-in a bit, but it is still a real project glove. Standard fit throughout.

name: Rawlings Pro Preferred 11.5" Baseball Glove: RPROS934-2BW
descriptionShort: The Pro Preferred PROS934, a premium kip-leather 11.5" infield glove built around a shallow I-web pocket and fast exchange play.
notes: This pattern keeps things compact and quick around the ball, making it an easy fit for middle-infield work. Pro Preferred kip leather feels a little smoother and lighter than steerhide, but it still needs time before it settles fully. Standard fit; no hand-size quirks.

name: Rawlings Pro Preferred 11.75" Baseball Glove: RPROS315-2MO
descriptionShort: The Pro Preferred PROS315, a premium kip-leather 11.75" infielder with a balanced pocket that can cover shortstop, third base, and utility work.
notes: The 11.75" size gives this more margin than a true small infield glove while still keeping the pocket controlled for quick transfers. Good fit for players who split time across the left side or want one premium glove for multiple dirt spots. Pro Preferred kip leather typically breaks in a bit faster than Heart of the Hide.

name: Rawlings Pro Preferred PROS314 11.5" Infield Glove
descriptionShort: The Pro Preferred PROS314 — premium kip leather in a shallow I-web infield pattern that delivers a softer feel and faster break-in than the HoH series.
notes: Pro Preferred uses kip leather which is softer and lighter than HoH steer hide — break-in is slightly more forgiving, closer to 3–4 weeks rather than 4–6. Same top-tier durability; just a different leather character.

name: Rawlings Pro Preferred PROS315 11.75" Infield Glove
descriptionShort: The Pro Preferred PROS315 — kip leather in an 11.75" I-web pattern with a medium pocket that adapts to corner infield and utility work.
notes: The balanced pocket depth of the PROS315 gives it more flexibility than shallow-pocket Pro Preferred variants — good for players splitting time between SS, 3B, and pitcher. Pro Preferred's kip leather typically breaks in 3–4 weeks.

name: Rawlings Pro Preferred PROS934 11.5" Infield Glove
descriptionShort: The Pro Preferred PROS934 — a shallow-pocket I-web infield pattern in kip leather for players who want quick transfer with pro-level construction.
notes: The PROS934 is a quick-transfer infield workhorse in the Pro Preferred lineup. Kip leather means slightly faster break-in than HoH (3–4 weeks). Standard fit; no sizing quirks.

name: Rawlings Pro Preferred Jacob deGrom Croc Skin 11.75" Baseball Glove: PROSJD48
descriptionShort: A premium 11.75" Pro Preferred pitcher's glove with kip leather, a closed two-piece look, and enough pocket depth for mound work or occasional left-side use.
notes: The closed web helps with pitch concealment, while the 11.75" size gives more coverage than a smaller middle-infield glove. Kip leather softens the feel compared with Heart of the Hide, but this still needs time and work before it feels fully formed. Standard fit and a balanced pocket.

name: Rawlings Pro Preferred Kris Bryant 12.25" Baseball Glove: PROSKB17C
descriptionShort: A premium 12.25" Pro Preferred utility glove with kip leather and an H-web pocket for players who split time between third base, utility, and shallow outfield.
notes: The 12.25" length makes this more versatile than a true infield-only glove, but it still stays controlled enough for players who live on the dirt. Good choice for corner infielders who need one glove to cover multiple spots. Pro Preferred kip leather brings a smoother feel, but break-in is still a real project.

name: Rawlings Pro Preferred 12" Baseball Glove: PROS206-12M
descriptionShort: A premium 12" Pro Preferred utility glove with kip leather and a balanced H-web pocket that can cover third base, pitcher, and multi-position work.
notes: This size lands in the middle of the utility range, giving more reach and pocket than an 11.75" glove without pushing fully into outfield territory. The H-web and neutral pocket shape make it easy to adapt across roles. Kip leather trims some weight and stiffness compared with Heart of the Hide, but not enough to call it game-ready.

name: Rawlings Pro Preferred 13" Baseball First Base Mitt: PROSBH3
descriptionShort: A premium 13" Pro Preferred first base mitt with kip leather, big scoop-friendly surface, and the cleaner feel that defines this line.
notes: The 13" size gives full adult first base coverage with strong catch security around the bag. Kip leather makes the mitt feel a little lighter and smoother than a steerhide equivalent, but it still needs a full break-in. Best for advanced first basemen who want a true long-term mitt.

descriptionShort: A premium 13" Pro Preferred first base mitt with kip leather, big scoop-friendly surface, and the cleaner feel that defines this line.

name: Rawlings Pro Preferred Anthony Rizzo 12.75" Baseball First Base Mitt: PROSAR44BB
descriptionShort: A premium 12.75" Pro Preferred first base mitt with kip leather and a slightly more compact profile than a traditional 13" adult mitt.
notes: The 12.75" length keeps the mitt a little quicker and more manageable than a full-size 13" first base model while still offering strong scoop help. Good fit for first basemen who want premium structure without the biggest possible surface area. Standard adult fit and a substantial break-in.

name: Rawlings R9 32" Baseball Catcher's Mitt: R9CMU-23BC
descriptionShort: A game-ready 32" baseball catcher's mitt with full-grain leather and a youth-friendly fit for developing backstops who need structure without a long break-in.
notes: The 32" size is a good entry point for younger catchers who want something more manageable than a full-size adult mitt. It runs slightly snug and closes more willingly than premium catcher's mitts, which helps with receiving and transfers. A strong step-up option for players getting serious behind the plate.

name: Rawlings R9 CM325 32.5" Catcher's Mitt
descriptionShort: A game-ready youth catcher's mitt with real leather construction at a price point that makes sense for players still growing into the position.
notes: The 32.5" size is ideal for catchers under 14. Leather is mid-grade but closes more willingly than premium options — a good starter mitt that won't fight back on every throw.

name: Rawlings R9 11.75" Baseball Glove: R9315-6CB
descriptionShort: A mid-grade 11.75" R9 infielder with an H-web, a balanced pocket, and a youth-friendly fit for players moving beyond entry-level leather.
notes: The 11.75" size gives more margin than a smaller middle-infield glove, making this a good fit for shortstop, third base, and utility infield use. It runs a little snug through the hand, which works well for teen players and smaller adults. Breaks in within a couple of weeks and does not fight the hand.

name: Rawlings R9 314 11.5" Infield Glove
descriptionShort: A mid-grade, youth-accessible infielder with a classic I-web and shallow pocket — reliable leather without the commitment of a premium break-in.
notes: Good step-up from entry-level for players 13–16 who want real leather at a reasonable price. Runs slightly snug — fits smaller youth and teen hands well but may feel tight on standard adult hands. Breaks in within 2 weeks.

name: Rawlings R9 12.75" Baseball Glove: R93029-6BP
descriptionShort: A mid-grade 12.75" R9 outfield glove with full-grain leather, extra reach, and a deep H-web pocket that is easy to handle for a glove this size.
notes: This is a good outfield step-up for teens and smaller-handed players who want real leather without a long premium break-in. The pocket is deep enough for secure catches on the move, and the slightly snug hand fit keeps the glove from feeling oversized. It can also cross into casual slowpitch if you prefer a smaller softball outfield glove.

name: Rawlings R9 12.75" Baseball Glove: R93029-6BG
descriptionShort: A mid-grade 12.75" R9 outfield glove with full-grain leather, extra reach, and a deep H-web pocket that is easy to handle for a glove this size.

name: Rawlings R9 Finger Shift 12.75" Baseball Glove: R96019BGFS
descriptionShort: A mid-grade 12.75" R9 outfield glove with a finger-shift back, deep pocket, and trap-style web for players who like a more wrapped, secure feel.
notes: The finger-shift construction changes the hand feel and can give the glove a more secure, settled connection once broken in. This pattern is built for outfield depth and catch security more than quick exchange work. Break-in is short by baseball standards and it can cross into casual slowpitch use.

name: Rawlings R9 11.75" Baseball Glove: R9205-4N
descriptionShort: A mid-grade 11.75" R9 pitcher's glove with a trap-style web and an approachable break-in for players who want mound coverage without a premium project.
notes: The 11.75" size gives enough length for concealment and occasional third base or utility work while still feeling manageable for teens. It runs slightly snug, which can help younger pitchers feel more connected to the glove. Break-in is short and the pocket settles faster than a premium steerhide model.

name: Rawlings R9 12" Baseball Glove: R9206-9SH
descriptionShort: A mid-grade 12" utility glove with a closed web and approachable break-in, built for pitcher, utility, and older youth players who want real leather without premium stiffness.
notes: The 12" size gives enough coverage for mound work and general utility use without feeling bulky. It runs slightly snug through the hand and closes more willingly than premium leather, so it suits teens and smaller adult hands well. Good step-up glove for players moving beyond starter gear.

name: Rawlings Renegade 31.5" Youth Catcher's Mitt: RCM315B
descriptionShort: An entry-level 31.5" youth catcher's mitt with soft full-grain leather and an easy close for younger backstops learning the position.
notes: The 31.5" size is a manageable starting point for youth catchers who need something they can actually squeeze. It is nearly game-ready out of the box and does not require much strength to close. Best for beginning players, recreational use, or younger catchers still learning receiving basics.

name: Rawlings Renegade 32.5" Youth Catcher's Mitt: RCM325B
descriptionShort: An entry-level 32.5" catcher's mitt with soft full-grain leather and a forgiving break-in for youth players growing into the position.
notes: The 32.5" size offers a little more surface area than the smaller Renegade mitt while still staying easy to handle. It comes nearly game-ready and closes with very little effort. A practical fit for newer catchers, rec leagues, or players who need an affordable backup mitt.

name: Rawlings Renegade 11.5" Youth Baseball First Base Mitt: R115FBM
descriptionShort: A youth-focused 11.5" first base mitt with soft full-grain leather and a nearly game-ready feel for beginning ballplayers.
notes: This is built for younger first basemen who need proper mitt shape without a hard break-in. The hand fit is approachable and the single-post design helps on basic scoops and short hops. Best for rec play, pre-teen players, or early position-specific use at first.

name: Rawlings Renegade 12.5" Baseball First Base Mitt: RFBMB
descriptionShort: An affordable 12.5" first base mitt with soft full-grain leather and easy break-in for recreational players who want true 1B construction.
notes: Nearly game-ready out of the box, this mitt is easy to open and close compared to stiffer leather models. The 12.5" size gives enough reach for routine scoops and stretch plays without feeling like a premium project. Good for rec leagues, backup first basemen, or players stepping into the position.

name: Rawlings REV1X 34" Baseball Catcher's Mitt: REVCM43-23CBP
descriptionShort: An elite 34" catcher's mitt that pairs REV1X hybrid construction with a deep receiving pocket and premium-level structure for advanced backstops.
notes: The larger 34" size is built for older catchers who want extra target area and strong catch security. It still breaks in faster than a traditional all-leather flagship, but this is not a soft starter mitt. Best for serious high school and adult players who want premium feel with a shorter break-in path.

name: Rawlings REV1X REV205 11.75" Infield Glove
descriptionShort: A hybrid-construction infielder with a balanced pocket — premium leather quality at mid-tier stiffness, making it one of the faster break-in options at this price point.
notes: The composite shell softens the break-in curve dramatically compared to traditional premium leather. You get a leatherQuality of 4 in a glove that breaks in like a 2 — genuinely one of the best value propositions in the catalog. Game-ready in 1–2 weeks.

notes: The REV3039 pattern is made for outfield range and dependable catch security, with enough length and pocket depth to handle full-speed play. It still needs break-in time, but the hybrid build keeps it more manageable than a traditional top-end outfield glove. Best for serious outfielders who want premium structure without the heaviest break-in load.

name: Rawlings Sandlot 11.5" Baseball Glove: S1150IS
descriptionShort: A game-ready recreational 11.5" infield glove with pre-oiled leather, an I-web, and enough structure for everyday league play without a long break-in.
notes: The Sandlot line is built to feel usable right away, and this model follows that formula. The 11.5" size keeps it quick for middle infield work while the leather still offers more substance than a soft starter glove. Good for rec leagues, adolescent players, or adults who want a casual infield glove without a project break-in.

name: Rawlings Sandlot 12.75" Baseball Glove: S1275HS
descriptionShort: A game-ready recreational 12.75" outfield glove with pre-oiled leather, extra reach, and a deep H-web pocket for secure catches.
notes: This model is ready quickly and does not ask for much break-in work before it becomes playable. The 12.75" size gives proper outfield length and enough pocket depth for routine catch security. A good fit for rec outfielders, casual adult play, or smaller slowpitch players who prefer a more manageable outfield glove.

name: Rawlings Sandlot 11.75" Baseball Glove: S1175MTS
descriptionShort: A game-ready recreational 11.75" utility glove with pre-oiled leather and a modified-trap style web for players splitting time between pitcher and the infield.
notes: The 11.75" size and slightly deeper shape make this more flexible than a true middle-infield glove. It comes nearly game-ready and is much easier to work than a premium leather model. Good for rec leagues, adolescent players, or adults who want a practical utility glove without much break-in effort.

name: Rawlings Sandlot SFM18 12.5" First Base Mitt
descriptionShort: A game-ready recreational first base mitt — soft leather, quick close, and solid scoop geometry for the player who wants to play first without making a major investment.
notes: The Sandlot line trades leather longevity for immediate usability — this mitt is ready out of the box. Perfect for recreational leagues or backup first basemen who don't want a premium break-in project. Standard fit throughout.

name: Rawlings Select Pro Lite 32" Catcher's Mitt
descriptionShort: A game-ready youth catcher's mitt designed so younger players can actually use it — soft leather, deep round pocket, and a close that doesn't require adult-level hand strength.
notes: The lightest, most accessible catcher's mitt in the catalog. Great first serious mitt for players 10–13. The slightly snug fit works well for smaller hands. No break-in needed — unbox and catch.

name: Rawlings Select Pro Lite 11.5" Youth Baseball Glove: SPL150BBCB
descriptionShort: A youth-friendly 11.5" infield glove with a pro-style I-web and soft, game-ready construction for players outgrowing smaller starter sizes.
notes: The 11.5" size works well for pre-teen to early-teen players who are starting to settle into the infield but still need an easy close. The Youth Pro Taper fit keeps the hand opening secure for smaller hands. A good bridge glove before stepping into a mid-grade leather model.

name: Rawlings Select Pro Lite SPL105 10.5" Infield Glove
descriptionShort: A first-game youth infield glove — very light, instantly usable, and built specifically for the youngest players learning to catch and throw.
notes: Designed for players 6–9. The lightweight shell and soft leather close easily even for developing hand strength. No break-in needed — this is a first-game glove.

name: Rawlings Select Pro Lite SPL110 11" Infield Glove
descriptionShort: A step up from the entry-level SPL line — still youth-friendly and game-ready, with a slightly larger I-web that introduces classic infield glove geometry.
notes: Good for players 8–11 who've outgrown their first starter glove but aren't ready for a leather break-in project. Closes easily out of the box. No real conditioning needed.

name: Rawlings Select Pro Lite SPL112 11.25" Infield Glove
descriptionShort: An I-web youth infielder — game-ready construction with just enough extra size for pre-teen to early-teen players making their first real positional commitment.
notes: The slight step up in size from smaller SPL models makes this more appropriate for players 10–13. Still closes easily, still no real break-in needed. Good bridge glove before committing to a first leather project.

name: Rawlings Select Pro Lite SPL150 11.5" Infield Glove
descriptionShort: The largest entry in this game-ready youth infield line — a starter glove designed for pre-teen and early-teen players who are outgrowing smaller youth sizes.
notes: At 11.5", this is approaching the lower end of adult infield sizing — appropriate for players 12–14 with developing hand size. Still soft enough to close without conditioning. The last step before stepping up to mid-grade leather.

name: Rawlings Select Pro Lite 12.25" Youth Baseball Glove: SPL1225MTN
descriptionShort: A youth-friendly 12.25" utility glove with a modified-trap style web and soft, game-ready construction for players covering multiple spots.
notes: This is one of the more flexible Select Pro Lite patterns, with enough size for outfield or general utility work on a youth diamond. The smaller hand opening keeps it manageable even though the glove runs larger. Best for young players who want one easy-closing glove to move around the field.

name: Rawlings Select Pro Lite 12" Youth Baseball Glove: SPL120AJG
descriptionShort: A game-ready 12" youth utility glove with an H-web and soft leather feel for players who need a little more reach without giving up control.
notes: The 12" size makes sense for youth outfield, utility, and older pre-teen all-position use. The Youth Pro Taper fit keeps the hand opening secure, and the soft construction makes it easy to close right away. Good for growing players who are ready to move past smaller beginner gloves.

name: Rawlings Select Pro Lite 12" Youth Baseball Glove: SPL120BHC
descriptionShort: A game-ready 12" youth glove with an H-web, soft leather, and enough reach for outfield or utility work on a youth field.
notes: This plays bigger than the smaller Select Pro Lite infield models, but it still stays manageable because the fit is built for youth hands. It closes easily out of the box and does not need meaningful break-in work. A strong option for players moving toward outfield or general utility roles.

name: Rawlings Select Pro Lite 11" Youth Baseball Glove: SPL110FTJ
descriptionShort: A game-ready 11" youth utility glove with soft leather and a secure Youth Pro Taper fit for younger players learning clean glove control.
notes: The 11" size keeps this manageable for developing hands, while the H-web adds a little more versatility than a traditional tiny starter glove. It closes easily right away and works well for players who are still bouncing around the field. Best for younger youth players who want an easy first serious glove.

name: Rawlings Select Pro Lite 11.25" Youth Baseball Glove: SPL112JH
descriptionShort: An easy-closing 11.25" youth infield glove with a pro-style I-web and a secure smaller-hand fit for developing middle infielders.
notes: The slight bump in size over an 11" model gives growing players a bit more margin without making the glove feel slow. The Youth Pro Taper fit helps it stay secure on smaller hands, and the soft build keeps break-in minimal. Good for pre-teen players starting to favor second base, shortstop, or general infield work.

name: Rawlings Select Pro Lite 11.5" Youth Baseball First Base Mitt: SPL115FF
descriptionShort: A youth-friendly 11.5" first base mitt with soft, game-ready construction for younger players learning the position with proper mitt shape.
notes: This mitt is built to be usable right away, which matters for young first basemen who are not ready for a stiff leather project. The smaller youth fit keeps it manageable through the hand while the single-post design helps with basic scoops. A strong first first-base mitt for youth league play.

name: Rawlings Sure Catch Mike Trout 11" Youth Baseball Glove: SC110MT
descriptionShort: A very soft 11" youth utility glove with a basket web and easy-close design for beginning players learning the basics.
notes: The Sure Catch heel cutout and soft leather make this one of the easiest gloves in the catalog for a young player to squeeze shut. The 11" size stays manageable for early baseball instruction and casual all-position use. Best as a true starter glove for beginners and younger youth players.

name: Rawlings Sure Catch Christian Yelich 11.5" Youth Baseball Glove: SC115CY
descriptionShort: A very soft 11.5" youth utility glove with an H-web and easy-close design for young players ready for a little more reach.
notes: This still has the same beginner-friendly Sure Catch formula, including the heel cutout that makes closure easier. The 11.5" size gives more flexibility for youth infield, outfield, and general utility use without becoming hard to handle. A good fit for young players who want a first glove that feels playable right away.

name: Wilson A1000 PF33 33" Baseball Catcher's Mitt: WBW10428233
descriptionShort: A mid-range 33" catcher's mitt with full-grain leather and a snugger PF-style fit for players who want real receiving shape without a premium break-in.
notes: The 33" size works well for baseball catchers who prefer a slightly more compact target, and the narrower hand fit helps it feel secure on smaller hands. Break-in is manageable for a mitt in this tier, usually around a couple of weeks of regular catch. A strong fit for developing catchers who want structure without a flagship price.

name: Wilson A1000 DW5 12" Infield Glove
descriptionShort: A versatile mid-range infielder with an H-web and slightly deeper pocket — adaptable across multiple infield spots without demanding a long break-in commitment.
notes: The 12" size and H-web make this one of the more flexible options in the mid-range lineup — plays credibly at 3B or in a utility role. Stiffness is manageable; expect 1–2 weeks before it settles into its shape.

name: Wilson A1000 1787 11.75" Infield Glove
descriptionShort: A mid-range 11.75" infielder with a classic I-web and medium pocket — real leather at an accessible price with a moderate break-in curve.
notes: Runs slightly narrow at the wrist compared to the premium equivalent of this pattern. Break-in is mild — about 2 weeks with regular play. A solid step up for players coming out of entry-level leather.

name: Wilson A1000 DP15 11.5" Infield Glove
descriptionShort: A snug, mid-range infielder with a classic shallow I-web pocket — built for 2B and SS players who want quick-release without going full premium.
notes: Runs very snug — narrower fit at both stall and wrist than most A1000s. If you're used to standard adult fit, you'll feel it immediately. Good for players with smaller hands or who prefer a locked-in feel.

name: Wilson A1000 1786 11.5" Baseball Glove: WBW103957115
descriptionShort: A mid-range 11.5" infield glove with a shallow I-web pocket and quick transfer feel for second base and shortstop players.
notes: The 1786 pattern is a classic middle-infield shape, and the A1000 version keeps the break-in approachable compared to Wilson's premium lines. It runs a little narrower than a roomy adult glove, but not overly tight. Good for travel, high school, or smaller-handed players who want real leather without a long project.

name: Wilson A1000 1788 11.25" Baseball Glove: WBW1042761125
descriptionShort: A mid-range 11.25" infield glove with a compact I-web shape built for fast transfers and precise glove control up the middle.
notes: The shorter 11.25" pattern keeps this glove quick and controlled for players who prefer a smaller infield look. Break-in is mild, usually around 1 to 2 weeks, and the hand fit stays manageable for smaller or average hands. Best for second basemen, young shortstops, and players who value speed over extra reach.

name: Wilson A1000 1975 11.75" Baseball Glove: WBW1039591175
descriptionShort: A mid-range 11.75" infield glove with a slightly deeper I-web pocket for shortstop, third base, and versatile infield work.
notes: The 1975 pattern offers more margin and catch security than a smaller middle-infield glove while still keeping transfers reasonably quick. It breaks in faster than Wilson's premium lines, making it easier to get game-ready. A good fit for travel and high school players who want a balanced left-side infield glove.

name: Wilson A1000 DP15 11.5" Baseball Glove: WBW100132115
descriptionShort: A snug, mid-range 11.5" infielder with a shallow I-web pocket, built for quick transfers and players who prefer a smaller-hand fit.
notes: The DP15 pattern runs narrow through the wrist and finger stalls, so it feels more locked in than a standard A1000. Break-in is moderate rather than heavy, and the shallow shape plays well at second base and shortstop. Best for smaller hands or anyone who likes a tighter infield fit.

name: Wilson A1000 DP15 11.5" Baseball Glove: WBW101442115
descriptionShort: A snug, mid-range 11.5" infielder with a shallow I-web pocket, built for quick transfers and players who prefer a smaller-hand fit.
notes: The DP15 pattern runs narrow through the wrist and finger stalls, so it feels more locked in than a standard A1000. Break-in is moderate rather than heavy, and the shallow shape plays well at second base and shortstop. Best for smaller hands or anyone who likes a tighter infield fit.

name: Wilson A1000 PF11 11" Baseball Glove: WBW10144111
descriptionShort: A compact 11" infield glove with a snug Pedroia-style fit and shallow I-web pocket for smaller-handed players who prioritize control and quick transfers.
notes: This is one of the smallest and quickest A1000 baseball patterns, so it fits best for younger teens, smaller adults, or anyone who prefers a very controlled glove. The tighter wrist and shorter finger feel help keep it secure. Break-in is light to moderate and much easier than a premium Wilson infielder.

name: Wilson A1000 1750 12.5" Outfield Glove
descriptionShort: A mid-range outfield glove in a proven H-web pattern — accessible break-in and enough catch security for everyday outfield work without a premium investment.
notes: Runs slightly narrow — standard for this line. Good break-in curve at stiffness 2; game-ready in 1–2 weeks with regular use. A solid choice for the outfielder who wants real leather without a premium project.

name: Wilson A1000 1750 12.5" Baseball Glove: WBW103964125
descriptionShort: A mid-range 12.5" outfield glove with full-grain leather, an H-web, and enough reach for everyday outfield work without a premium break-in.
notes: The 1750 pattern gives proper outfield length and dependable catch security while staying more approachable than Wilson's top-tier lines. Expect a moderate break-in rather than a long project. A solid fit for travel, high school, or recreational outfielders who want real leather with less work.

name: Wilson A1000 KP92 12.5" Outfield Glove
descriptionShort: A mid-range outfield glove with a modified trap web and deep pocket — approachable break-in and enough catch security for everyday outfield work.
notes: Good option for players who want an outfield glove without a premium time commitment. Modified trap gives good ball visibility while maintaining catch security. Runs slightly narrow — standard for this lineup.

name: Wilson A1000 PF1892 12.25" Outfield Glove
descriptionShort: A mid-range outfield glove with a single-post web and moderate pocket depth — accessible break-in and solid construction for everyday outfield work.
notes: Slightly narrow fit at the wrist — standard for this line. The single-post web is an unusual outfield choice that offers good ball transfer visibility. Breaks in in 1–2 weeks.

name: Wilson A1000 PF1892 12.25" Outfield Glove
descriptionShort: A quick-ready outfield glove with a single-post web — softer leather makes this one of the most accessible mid-range outfield options off the shelf.
notes: Game-ready in days rather than weeks. The single-post web is unusual for outfield but gives excellent ball transfer visibility for players who lose the ball in sun or lights on routine fly balls. Runs slightly narrow.

name: Wilson A1000 B23 12" Baseball Glove: WBW10427912
descriptionShort: A mid-range 12" utility glove with a closed web and balanced pocket for pitchers and players moving between the mound and infield.
notes: The B23 pattern offers enough size for mound use and general utility coverage without feeling oversized. It breaks in more quickly than premium Wilson gloves, so it is easier to get field-ready in a short window. A practical option for pitchers, third basemen, and players who want one glove for multiple roles.

name: Wilson A1000 CK22 11.75" Baseball Glove: WBW1042781175
descriptionShort: A mid-range 11.75" utility glove with a closed web and balanced pocket for pitchers, shortstops, and versatile infield use.
notes: This pattern sits between a true pitcher glove and a standard infield model, which makes it useful for players who move around the dirt. The closed web keeps it mound-friendly, and the 11.75" size adds a little more margin than a smaller middle-infield glove. Break-in is manageable and well suited to travel or high school players.

name: Wilson A1000 1620 12.5" First Base Mitt
descriptionShort: A mid-range first base mitt with real leather geometry and accessible break-in — a practical choice for the player who wants proper 1B construction without a premium commitment.
notes: Runs slightly narrow — standard for this line. Break-in is moderate; 2–3 weeks of regular use and this mitt settles into its shape well. Good option for recreational or high school 1B players stepping up from entry-level.

name: Wilson A1000 1620 12.5" First Base Mitt
descriptionShort: An accessible, moderately-stiff first base mitt for players who want real leather geometry at a mid-range price.
notes: Softer hide than a full Pro Stock model — opens up in 2–3 weeks of regular use. Runs slightly narrow at the wrist; if you wear larger gloves, consider sizing up.

name: Wilson A1000 PF1612 12" Baseball First Base Mitt: WBW10428412
descriptionShort: A mid-range 12" first base mitt with full-grain leather and a snugger PF-style fit for players who want proper 1B shape in a more compact package.
notes: The slightly smaller size and tighter hand fit make this a strong option for younger first basemen, smaller-handed players, or anyone who prefers a more controlled first base mitt. It still offers real scoop geometry without the long break-in of a premium model. Best for travel ball and developing first basemen.

name: Wilson A1000 SuperSnakeSkin CM33 33" Baseball Catcher's Mitt: WBW10396633
descriptionShort: A mid-range 33" catcher's mitt that pairs full-grain leather with a lighter SuperSkin back for easier handling and a manageable break-in.
notes: The 33" size gives a compact, controllable target, and the lighter SuperSkin back keeps the mitt from feeling as heavy through long innings. It still has enough structure to feel like a real catcher's mitt, but it opens up faster than a premium model. Good for developing catchers who want lighter feel without giving up mitt shape.

name: Wilson A1000 SuperSkin PF11 11" Baseball Glove: WBW10395511
descriptionShort: A snug, mid-range 11" infielder with a lightweight SuperSkin back and Pedroia-style fit for smaller hands and quick infield control.
notes: The narrow wrist opening and shorter finger feel make this one of the more secure A1000 fits for younger players or smaller adults. It breaks in quickly and closes easily, so it gets field-ready without much work. The lighter back helps it stay quick through transfers.

name: Wilson A2000 AW28 34" Catcher's Mitt
descriptionShort: Wilson's top-tier catcher's mitt in elite leather — deep pocket, two-piece closed web, and the break-in commitment that serious receivers know is part of the deal.
notes: Plan on 4–6 weeks of real conditioning work. The two-piece closed web gives excellent pitch framing feedback once broken in. Standard fit geometry throughout; sizing is predictable. One of the best catching mitts available.

name: Wilson A2000 Classic M23 33.5" Baseball Catcher's Mitt: WBW102093335
descriptionShort: A premium 33.5" catcher's mitt in Wilson's Pro Stock leather, built for receivers who want a deep pocket, classic shape, and long-term durability.
notes: This is a real A2000 catcher's mitt, so expect a firm start and a full 4 to 6 week break-in. The 33.5" size gives a large, secure target, and the standard hand fit should feel familiar to most adult catchers. Best for players who want structure that lasts.

name: Wilson A2000 PP05 11.5" Infield Glove
descriptionShort: The A2000 PP05 — Pro Stock leather with an H-web and shallow pocket for infielders who prefer the H-web's broader coverage over the I-web's pure transfer speed.
notes: The H-web variant of Wilson's shallow-pocket infield lineup gives slightly more coverage on hard-hit ground balls. Same A2000 construction and break-in (4–5 weeks). Standard fit throughout.

name: Wilson A2000 PP05 11.5" Baseball Glove: WBW101386115
descriptionShort: The A2000 PP05 — Pro Stock leather with an H-web and shallow pocket for infielders who prefer the H-web's broader coverage over the I-web's pure transfer speed.
notes: The H-web variant of Wilson's shallow-pocket infield lineup gives slightly more coverage on hard-hit ground balls. Same A2000 construction and break-in, usually about 4 to 5 weeks. Standard fit throughout.

descriptionShort: The A2000 1975 — a medium-pocket I-web infielder in Pro Stock leather for players who want the same build quality in a slightly larger and more versatile pattern.
notes: The 1975 is Wilson's answer for corner infielders or utility players who find a shallow pocket too limiting. The medium depth gives more room on wide throws. Same A2000 break-in timeline (4–5 weeks) and standard fit.

name: Wilson A2000 1786 11.5" Infield Glove
descriptionShort: The most iconic infield glove pattern in Pro Stock leather — a shallow I-web pocket that has defined quick-transfer fielding for generations of competitive infielders.
notes: This is the benchmark for premium infield gloves. Standard fit geometry means sizing is predictable; the shallow pocket rewards commitment to quick hands. Plan on 4–6 weeks of break-in. Worth every minute.

descriptionShort: The A2000 1786 in Pro Stock leather — the most recognized infield I-web pattern in Wilson's lineup, with a shallow pocket designed for players who live by transfer speed.
